geography.description: OPTIONAL for MM — present only for the minority of beats that use a real map/aerial. A no-map chapter omits this entirely (RULE 3: maps are not central).

setting.description: Non-geographic place for medium/micro scenes that never touch a basemap (a boardroom, an alley, a ledger cell). Backs spatial_target.region_id for off-map action cues (RULE 3 + spatial sync).

scene_anchors.description: Named on-screen regions/actors a cue can target via spatial_target.region_id.

through_line.description: OPTIONAL brand-spine declaration — the recurring through-line this video returns to at every chapter close (the per-video replacement for the hardcoded all-presidents map). Declarative only here; the gate (qa_spine_consistency) enforces presence + per-chapter consistency.

primitive.description: the primitive name (must be a member of the layer_action primitive enum) that renders the recurring spine

close_phase_kinds.description: phase_kind values that count as a chapter close where the spine must appear; defaults to [return_to_map, chapter_outro] if omitted

consistency_keys.description: param paths that must stay identical across chapters (e.g. roster ids/positions for a map; row order for a timeline)

phase_state.description: Cross-phase continuity contract (RULE 3: map fields optional; MM uses scene/subject fields). qa_continuity compares only the fields that are present on BOTH sides of a boundary.

basemap_tier.description: OPTIONAL — only when a map is present.

beat_id.description: Unique within its chapter, no dots/whitespace — a dot mis-binds the compiler's <chapter>.<beat>.<prim>.<idx> cue ids and <chapter>.<beat> scene-graph filenames; the compiler also rejects a duplicate beat_id in a chapter.

intent.description: Human-readable description of what this beat does narratively.

shot_scale.description: Shot scale (map-OPTIONAL): macro=establishing (may be a map, an aerial, or a wide noir scene), medium=the scene where action happens, micro=close-up on a face/object/document. Most MM visuals live at medium+micro.

animation_tier.description: Render mode chosen per shot for best fit: 2d=flat-segmented noir scene (HyperFrames/GSAP — chiaroscuro/ledger idiom), 3d=bird's-eye diorama. MUST be resolved for hero / medium-scale beats (qa_render_directive); absent = not yet chosen.

hero.description: True for a hero shot. A hero shot (or any medium-scale scene, 2D or 3D) MUST carry a render_directive AND a resolved animation_tier (qa_render_directive).

render_directive.description: Path (relative to project root) to this beat's Render Directive .md. MANDATORY for hero beats and every medium-scale scene (2D or 3D); the rich per-shot brief handed to the animator agent.

emotion_face.description: RULE 4 — an emotional medium/close-up human FACE for this beat. When present, the beat MUST render a face primitive (face_closeup/face_reaction/face_medium/reaction_cut). Sourced from a real still/clip first, else generated (Nano Banana).

experimental.description: True for non-enum'd primitives (max 2 per phase). Requires rationale field if true.

phrase.description: Exact contiguous Whisper word/phrase from the VO. In a FINAL render this MUST resolve (qa_drift); inventing a time via fallback is a hard fail.

offset_ms.description: Milliseconds before/after the anchor word. Applied identically by the compiler and the drift gates (shared tokenizer).

occurrence_index.description: Disambiguates a phrase matching multiple runs in scope (silent first-occurrence binding is banned — qa_drift).

near_s.description: Alternative disambiguator: approximate master-seconds of the intended occurrence.

fallback_absolute_s.description: Time-only fallback. LEGAL only with fallback_reason set AND a structural (non-final) build; a USED fallback in a final render is a hard fail (qa_drift).

layer.description: Z-order layer. See design intel doc Section 15.

params.description: Primitive-specific parameters; compiler enforces shape. Notable shapes: migration_arrow.params.character_icon = {asset_id|icon|sprite|label, ...} declares a transportation/character icon that travels the route polyline (a rider on horseback), and migration_arrow.params.no_icon_rationale (string) documents a deliberately icon-less route (qa_migration_icon). all_presidents_pins.params = {pins:[{id, anchor_id|lat+lon, president?}], current:<pin id>} renders an all-presidents map (ONE possible through-line spine, not the mandated one) with `current` in color and the rest dimmed. Whatever spine primitive a video DECLARES via top-level through_line, the params at its declared consistency_keys must be deep-equal across chapters (qa_spine_consistency).

drift_budget_ms.description: May only TIGHTEN the default per-cue-type budget (qa_cue_drift), never loosen it.

cue_anchor.description: Per-CUE time anchor (overrides the beat's start for this layer). Required for the 2nd+ time-distinct action in a beat so each action lands on its own narrated word (qa_cue_drift).

time_distinct.description: True if this layer fires at its own narrated moment, not the beat start.

placement.description: on_action = must sit on the narrated thing (needs spatial_target); chrome = intentionally action-independent UI (year card, badge, citation).

spatial_target.description: WHERE on the frame this cue lands. Provide exactly one of anchor_id / region_id / target_px. Required for every visible non-chrome action cue (qa_spatial_anchor); the compiler stamps the resolved pixel onto the cuelist for rendered-pixel verification (qa_visual_alignment).

region_id.description: A named region/actor in the shot scene_graph or the setting.scene_anchors index.

target_px.description: [x,y] or [x,y,w,h] authored pixel for a 2D/3D scene.

anchor_color.description: [r,g,b] of the cue's rendered content, for the COLOR_TOL render-verification path on noir frames (qa_visual_alignment).
